title: What Lilith Wants REBUILD SPOILERS
wikitext:
[]: What Lilith Wants [REBUILD SPOILERS]             Quote Post by Tumorhead » Tue Mar 25, 2014 7:45 pm Hello all! After digging through the wiki recently, I began thinking about the point that the struggle between the Angels and Lilith is about. Note that this takes in to account mostly Rebuild stuff, but I've of a mind that NGE, EoE and NTE are a single related work. Let me know what you guys think! I'd love any additional thoughts. -------- In the Judaic tradition, Lilith was made by God the same way as - and thus equal to - Adam. She was made to be Adam's helper, confidante, wife, but she refused to "lie beneath him". This is a double entendre- She refused to have sex with him, AND to be subjugated and treated as lesser, and thus left. Legend has it that she births 100 demons a day to kill the sons of Adam out of spite. She brings us themes of fecundity, death, and defiance. What happens when we superimpose biblical Adam and Lilith's wants on to Evangelion's Adams and Lilith? The conflict in Evangelion is centered around the fight between the two forms of life. These two "genders" are those who ate the Fruit of Life - Angels/Adams, and those who ate the Fruit of Knowledge - Lilith/lilim (aka humans). Adams are blessed with eternal life and free self-change. Lilim are blessed with knowledge but cannot change themselves, and cursed with death. It's a good idea to ask - what do lilim have that Angels do not? One, they outnumber Angels billions to one. Only 12 angels. They also have knowledge. Does that mean the angels, conversely, have no minds, no free will? (yes Kaworu is a problem. But, he is acting LIKE a lilin. This is for further exploration later). The Angels/Adams are trying to get to Lilith. What for? The Judaic legend suggests they want to lie with her and begin a new race. But that doesn't sound so bad, right? In Rebuild of Evangelion, it's said that Nerv has a covenant with Lilith, and Nerv's half is to kill the Angels/Adams. They're defending her. Why would Lilith want Nerv- humans - to kill the Angels/Adams? She does not want to lie - aka mate with - an Adam . It seems she can't do it herself, and she needs the humans to do so. We never see her with the powers of the Adams, blasting beams and whatnot. Lilith doesn't want to be the wife of Adam. That's the basis for the whole Lilith mythos in it's simplest form. in Eva, she doesn't want the Angels to touch her. Why wouldn't Lilith want to mate with Adam? She was told she needed to be subjugated, and considered worth less, than her male counterpart. In Evangelion, lilim DEFINITELY seem less-than the mighty Adams. However, if we follow her reasoning, Lilith does not think she is - or her children! Lilith thinks her children are just as valuable, important, and worthwhile as the allpowerful, eternal Adams. But how would a mating with Lilith be bad, if it will birth a new race? Rebuild gives us the answer: old life must be sacrificed for this new life to be born. Lilins must be destroyed, HUMANITY MUST END, for this new life to emerge. Lilith doesn't want to sacrifice all of humanity to do that! The Adams are fine with killings humans, obviously. The Adams must not see humanity as worthy of anything. A big theme in Eva is accepting that human existance as it is- in separate bodies but with each other - is worth struggling to understand, to bear the true worth of humanity - the love, the connectedness between individuals that can't exist in either mindless beings or unified souls. Shinji in EoE accepts that he wants all souls to be in separate bodies, that he loves the world and how it is, and is okay with the hardships, that human life is worth living as a human. There is something humans have that cannot exist without them as they are. Starts with L and ends in Ove? (I don't think it' JUST love, it's the whole human experience as beautiful and worthwhile). We can compare two characters. Gendo Ikari is a lilim who wants to cast off the lilim mode of life and 1) in EoE unite all souls in to one and end isolation and loneliness at the sacrifice of individuality and 2) in Rebuild to uplift humans in to powerful Adam-like, Fruit of Life bearing, eternally living super beings. Both of these cast off the curse of death and loss. Kaworu is an Angel, and Adam, who learns to live with lilim, and learns they're special in their own way. His aliegence seems to shift from working for the Angels, to working for Gendo to create the super beings, to the the lilim - at least, for Shinji. He learns that lilim have their own traits that are worth fighting for - connectedness, joy. The human experience should not be tossed aside in favor of supposedly "better" lifeforms. ------- RELATED 1 Mythos-wise, Evangelion does a frame-shift. Obviously Evangelion's Lilith and Adam are not humans, so it's easy to see how humans in Evangelion do not equate to the humans (Adam and Lilith) in the mythos. Lilim are another name for demons. So lilim are less than the blessed children of God, creations not meant to be, unholy. To be "unto God" (as Kaji says in Rebuild) is to be the same level as Adam and Lilith, who were made in THE IMAGE of God. So uplifting humans via Instrumentality (in Rebuild) is to make them as powerful as Lilith and Adam. THIS is the new race made from the union of Adam and Lilith. They may have both the Fruit of Life and the Fruit of Knowledge and be amazing super beings in eternal bliss. but EVEN SO, Lilith doesn't want to destroy her children to birth them! Indeed, the process happening on Earth in Evangelion seems to be the reproductive process of these mighty beings. It makes sense that the Angels and Adams and Lilith are the sexual organs/generation of the ultimate beings. Thus Lilith is defying her duty to a greater cause. RELATED 2 Why would Lilith need to be pinned up in Central Dogma? Concidering her LCL is "primordial fluid", once lilim-based life was established it didn't need to continue to flow. She initiated life and then her job was done, but her fecundity may be unable to stop without outside measures. She could've orchestrated for herself to be contained so her children could live. It's possible she's so fecund she can't truly die, or it takes an Impact to do so. RELATED 3 Themes of conception, gestation and birth symbolism abound. This fits perfectly if the central conflict is fighting over wanting to give birth. The two sides are two genders. Giant fetus Angel 12 in 3.33. Adam as a fetus on Gendo's hand. The Black and White Moon as eggs. Unit 00 pregnant with that one angel in NGE. Mainly I wanted to point out: AAA Wunder is likely built around an Adam. Mark .09, and Adam Vessel, is "it's true Master". Thus, is AAA Wunder is MALE Adam, no wonder it looks like a Giant Sperm , even with a power core where the nucleus would be.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Mar 25, 2014 9:20 pm The angels basically are sperm. The resemblance of many of them to viruses or molecular structures isn't coincidental either. They are all trying to fertilize the black moon/egg, of with Lilith is the nucleus. Not to toot my own horn, but I would suggest you have a look at this thread: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/15461/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-is-an-Occult-Grimoire/ specifically the associations behind the name "Lilith".

[]:             Quote Post by Tumorhead » Tue Mar 25, 2014 10:20 pm Hi Chuckman, I've not been on the forums for awhile so I'm reading all your posts now! I love your analyses I've read so far, enjoying them greatly. Definitely agree with your observations. The Mother Goddess angle hasn't been one I've looked at Eva through until now and it's really broadening my enjoyment of the work (which I didn't think was possible, ha!).

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Mar 25, 2014 11:29 pm There's no indication that Lillith was supposed to have anything in comon with her mythic name sake. Anno probably took it to allude to how she and Adam are equal things compaed to the 'derived' EVA, just like their mythological namesakes were made from dust at the same time, in contrast to the whole rib story that resulted in Eve. The kaballah stuff is just windowdressing, at most intended to strike at commonly found archetypes and associations an subtext, not where you would look for actual meanings. The allusions to archetypes like the "chained mother goddess" may be appealing (although a honest look at "pagan history" reveals the idea to be as much as a myth as the notions/fantasies that "men lived in free, uncomplicated spartan heroism untill the women came and took it all away"), but let's take a good look at what we actually see, which is no indication that the SoLs "want" or "experience" anything at all. Even before she got captured, de-souled and coverted into an LCL harvesting factory, there was never any indication that she did anything other than ooze LCL for the last billion years; It's not like she, say, walked over to where adam was and kicked him off the planet with her magic AT-field powers or anything else that would suggest "protecting" her children in a way that would mean anything to us mammals. She's certainly anthropomorphic enough, and as far as we can tell may have had a soul roughly comparable to that of a Lillim, with a potential to act and feel not that different from them (see mid-series episodes), but what sort of experience/self-reflection could you have if you spent your whole existence in a cave, oozing LCL, with no interactions at all? It would be kind of like the "white room"/instrumentality/"world of freedom" Shinji experinces in ep 26, nothing to contrast against to form an ego. In any case, regardless of what one might speculate, Rei discribes Lillith's presence as utterly alien, scary and inacessible to her. From what little evidence we have at all, you could just as easily spin a narrative about how she sees her progeny as failed and longs to reset them... It gets even worse when you look at the CI, wich basically makes Lillith little other than an organic terraforming device crafted by the FAR. Why would Lilith need to be pinned up in Central Dogma?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Mar 26, 2014 2:10 am Lilith and Adam want the same thing: For their own children to exist to prosper. But given their origins, and how they interact with each other as Rei and Kaworu, they don't seem to have any sort of animosity or jealousy of each other. Like, they're basically coworkers, and that their kids ended up having to share a planet is entirely a fluke and an enmity that exists in their progenity, but not with each other.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Mar 26, 2014 4:00 am Still there's an interesting point about Lilith in Rebuild : Tumorhead wrote: In Rebuild of Evangelion, it's said that Nerv has a covenant with Lilith, and Nerv's half is to kill the Angels/Adams. They're defending her. Why would Lilith want Nerv- humans - to kill the Angels/Adams? She does not want to lie - aka mate with - an Adam . It seems she can't do it herself, and she needs the humans to do so. We never see her with the powers of the Adams, blasting beams and whatnot. Lilith doesn't want to be the wife of Adam. That's the basis for the whole Lilith mythos in it's simplest form. in Eva, she doesn't want the Angels to touch her. Why wouldn't Lilith want to mate with Adam? She was told she needed to be subjugated, and considered worth less, than her male counterpart. In Evangelion, lilim DEFINITELY seem less-than the mighty Adams. However, if we follow her reasoning, Lilith does not think she is - or her children! Lilith thinks her children are just as valuable, important, and worthwhile as the allpowerful, eternal Adams. But how would a mating with Lilith be bad, if it will birth a new race? Rebuild gives us the answer: old life must be sacrificed for this new life to be born. Lilins must be destroyed, HUMANITY MUST END, for this new life to emerge. Lilith doesn't want to sacrifice all of humanity to do that! The Adams are fine with killings humans, obviously. The Adams must not see humanity as worthy of anything.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Mar 26, 2014 9:13 am It's worth bearing in mind that in Rebuild, the Third Impact/HIP seems to have an entirely different goal or purpose than in the TV series. Like, conflating the two series is completely useless because there are too many differences to use anything in reference to the other.
